name: North Holland (Netherlands)

colors.symbolism: shared between the West Frisian and Holland traditions
from the historic colours of Holland
from the historic colours of West Friesland

description: Three equal horizontal bands of yellow (top), red, and blue.
history: Adopted by the provincial council on 22 October 1958. The flag merges the colour traditions of West Friesland and Holland, the two regions whose union forms the modern province.

features.symbolism: the colours combine West Frisian (blue and yellow) and Holland (red and yellow) traditions, reflecting the province's two historical components
